title: bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. planiculmis
intro: Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. planiculmis, also known as sea club-rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found growing in wetlands, salt marshes, and coastal areas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infections. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote digestion, reduce fever, and support the immune system.
